title: Box office buzz: Best quarter in five years fuels hopes for Hollywood comeback
content:
[Woman holding popcorn and movie ticket]
Hakase_/iStock via Getty Images

The U.S. box office sizzled in Q1, with revenue climbing 25% year over year to $1.8B and surpassing analysts’ expectations. Notably, the quarterly tally marked the highest in five years, though it remains below pre-pandemic levels, according to analysis from Macquarie.

Leading the Q1 results were Sony Pictures’ (SONY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SONY]) _Project Hail Mary_ ($177M), continued momentum from 20th Century Studios’ (FOX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FOX]) Avatar: Fire and Ash ($154M), and Illumination/Universal’s (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) Hoppers ($142M).

Looking ahead, Q2 began on a strong note with Nintendo (NTDOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTDOY]) and Universal’s (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) _The Super Mario Galaxy Movie,_ which opened April 1 and has already grossed over $200M domestically. The momentum could continue through an eventful slate that includes Lionsgate’s (LION [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LION]) _Michael_ (April 24), 20th Century Studios’ (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]) _The Devil Wears Prada 2_ (May 1), Lucasfilm's (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]) _The Mandalorian and Grogu_ (May 22), Paramount's (PSKY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSKY]) _Disclosure Day_ (June 12), Pixar's (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]) _Toy Story 5_ (June 19), and Warner Bros.' (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]) _Supergirl_ (June 26).

This week, the buzz in the film industry will be surrounding CinemaCon on April 13-16 in Las Vegas. Macquarie analyst Chad Beynon and his team expect key topics to be studio consolidation, the 45-day theatrical window amid Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) pressure, the encouraging film slate, and capital expenditure spending.

"Theatre company management teams continue to be positive on box office growth in '26 given the strength of the film slate and ongoing demand for premium formats, which should benefit average ticket price (ATP). Additionally, F&B and merchandise sales continue to see positive trends as well," highlighted Beynon.

The firm maintained its $9.7B industry box office forecast for the full year, which would be the best mark for the industry since 2019.

title: Benchmark updates its best ideas list
content:
[Close Up Image Of A Stock Market Graph]
solarseven/iStock via Getty Images

Benchmark Equity Research on Tuesday released its quarterly update to the firm’s “best ideas” list, featuring 29 high-conviction, catalyst-driven stock picks selected by the company’s analysts.

The April 2026 refresh includes three notable additions to the list: IMAX Corp. (IMAX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IMAX]), CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), and Lifeway Foods (LWAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LWAY]). title: 1 Small-Cap Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 We Ignore
content:
Small-cap stocks can be incredibly lucrative investments because their lack of analyst coverage leads to frequent mispricings. However, these businesses (and their stock prices) often stay small because their subscale operations make it harder to expand their competitive moats.

Luckily for you, our mission at StockStory is to help you make money and avoid losses by sorting the winners from the losers. Keeping that in mind, here is one small-cap stock that could be the next big thing and two best left ignored.

Two Small-Cap Stocks to Sell:

FTI Consulting (FCN)

Market Cap: $5.50 billion

With a team of experts deployed across 30+ countries to tackle complex business challenges, FTI Consulting (NYSE:FCN) is a global business advisory firm that helps organizations manage change, mitigate risk, and resolve disputes across financial, legal, operational, and regulatory matters.

Why Do We Think Twice About FCN?

4.2% annual revenue growth over the last two years was slower than its business services peers Earnings growth underperformed the sector average over the last two years as its EPS grew by just 6.8% annually Free cash flow margin dropped by 7.9 percentage points over the last five years, implying the company became more capital intensive as competition picked up

FTI Consulting’s stock price of $183.33 implies a valuation ratio of 19.7x forward P/E. Read our free research report to see why you should think twice about including FCN in your portfolio, it’s free.

DigitalBridge (DBRG)

Market Cap: $2.83 billion

Transforming from a traditional real estate investor to a digital-focused powerhouse in 2021, DigitalBridge Group (NYSE:DBRG) is a global digital infrastructure investment firm that manages capital and operates assets across data centers, cell towers, fiber networks, and edge infrastructure.

Why Do We Avoid DBRG?

Products and services are facing significant end-market challenges during this cycle as sales have declined by 68.3% annually over the last five years Earnings per share have contracted by 14.1% annually over the last two years, a headwind for returns as stock prices often echo long-term EPS performance EBITDA losses may force it to accept punitive lending terms or high-cost debt

At $15.44 per share, DigitalBridge trades at 2.2x forward P/E. If you’re considering DBRG for your portfolio, see our FREE research report to learn more.

One Small-Cap Stock to Buy:

IMAX (IMAX)

Market Cap: $2.17 billion

Originally developed for World Expo '67 in Montreal as an innovative projection system, IMAX (NYSE:IMAX) provides proprietary large-format cinema technology and systems that deliver immersive movie experiences with enhanced image quality and sound.

Story Continues

Why Do We Love IMAX?

Annual revenue growth of 24.5% over the past five years was outstanding, reflecting market share gains this cycle Free cash flow margin expanded by 24.3 percentage points over the last five years, providing additional flexibility for investments and share buybacks/dividends Returns on capital are increasing as management’s prior bets are starting to bear fruit

IMAX is trading at $40.13 per share, or 23.7x forward P/E. Is now the time to initiate a position? See for yourself in our comprehensive research report, it’s free.

High-Quality Stocks for All Market Conditions

ONE MORE THING: Top 5 Growth Stocks. The biggest stock winners almost always had one thing in common before they ran. Revenue growing like crazy. Meta. CrowdStrike. Broadcom. Our AI flagged all three. They returned 315%, 314%, and 455%, respectively.

Find out which 5 stocks it's flagging for this month — FREE.  Get Our Top 5 Growth Stocks for Free HERE.

Stocks that made our list in 2020 include now familiar names such as Nvidia (+1,326% between June 2020 and June 2025) as well as under-the-radar businesses like the once-micro-cap company Tecnoglass (+1,754% five-year return). Find your next big winner with StockStory today.
